title: Budapest, 17-21 Nov 2025
subtitle: A long weekend exploring Budapest's backstreets, thermal baths, and golden hour along the Danube.

images.bannerAlt: Budapest Parliament from Pest embankment at golden hour

day2.alt: Parliament from Pest embankment at golden hour

day3.alt: Fisherman's Bastion side streets and arches, quiet angles
Buda embankment long view to Parliament

day4.alt: Szentendre Fő tér cobbles and Danube promenade
Liberty Bridge silhouettes at blue hour

translation.commonPhrases: Szia (Hello) • Köszönöm (Thank you) • Mennyibe kerül? (How much?)

airportTransfer.name: 100E Airport Express
airportTransfer.details: Terminal 2 to Deák Ferenc tér, approx 2,200 HUF. Buy on BudapestGO app or BKK machines.
airportTransfer.note: Special 100E single ticket only, not valid on other lines. Allow 90 mins buffer at BUD.

gettingAround.modes: Walking, metro, trams, official airport buses only.
gettingAround.appNote: Use BudapestGO app for tickets and journey planning. HÉV H5 to Szentendre needs suburban add-on beyond city limits.

weather.summary: Short daylight, golden hour around 16:00. Chilly single digits, near-freezing nights, possible showers and wind. Pack layers.

packing: Layers over bulk, compact umbrella, scarf and beanie, light gloves, trainers with grip for damp cobbles, microfibre towel for baths, spare phone battery for the cold.
farewell: Hope you had an amazing time in Budapest, Ryan!
footer: Have an amazing trip to Budapest!

Fricska 2.0.name: Dobrumba
Fricska 2.0.why: Stylish Levantine, comfortable seating with aromatic share plates.

Boutiq'Bar.name: Tuk Tuk Bar
Boutiq'Bar.why: Travel vibe with edge, balanced crowd, tight cocktail builds.

Café Kör.name: Két Szerecsen
Café Kör.why: Stylish bistro done right, duck breast and seafood risotto.

Rosenstein Vendéglő.name: Pest-Buda Bistro
Rosenstein Vendéglő.why: Castle district comfort with warm service and intimate room.

Pest-Buda Bistro.name: Café Kör
Pest-Buda Bistro.why: Old-world Pest, civilised, bookable, dependable classic.

Két Szerecsen.name: Dobrumba
Két Szerecsen.why: Levantine aromatic plates in stylish setting.

Hotsy Totsy Budapest.name: Boutiq'Bar
Hotsy Totsy Budapest.why: Benchmark mixing, moody room, serious drinks.

Tuk Tuk Bar.name: Hotsy Totsy Budapest
Tuk Tuk Bar.why: Vinyl, low light, crafted classics with edge.

sections.title: Airport → Klauzál tér 6
sections.content: Route: **100E Airport Express** to **Deák Ferenc tér** then walk about 12 mins to the door. Use the 100E single ticket. If rain is heavy, allow 5 extra mins for slower pavements.

sections.title: Relaxed first evening photowalk
sections.content: Loop: Klauzál tér → Rákóczi út side streets → Király utca textures → Gozsdu passages neon → Kazinczy utca murals → home.
sections.wet: Budapest Pinball Museum for late opening and warm indoor atmosphere.

sections.heading: Dinner at Fricska 2.0
sections.description: Modern Hungarian bistro, polished but not pretentious, near base. Duck leg with red cabbage is exceptional.

sections.heading: Cocktails at Boutiq'Bar
sections.description: Benchmark mixing, moody room, serious drinks, proper seating. Try a Basil Smash.

activities.name: Lipótváros grid to Kossuth Lajos tér

sections.content: **10:30** Klauzál Market and nearby lanes for local life and textures.
sections.wet: House of Music Hungary foyer and interior forms.

sections.heading: Lunch near Basilica at Café Kör
sections.description: Old-world, civilised, bookable. Veal paprikash is excellent.

sections.content: **14:00** Lipótváros grid to **Kossuth Lajos tér** with **Szabadság tér** and Hold utca facades.
sections.wet: Parliament interior tour if available that afternoon.

sections.title: 15:30-16:45 Golden hour
sections.content: Pest embankment north of Parliament and **Margit híd** perspectives.

sections.heading: Dinner at Rosenstein Vendéglő
sections.description: Hungarian-Jewish family institution with depth and genuine hospitality. Goose liver or cholent are standouts.

sections.heading: Cocktails at Hotsy Totsy Budapest
sections.description: Vinyl, low light, crafted classics with edge without attitude. Try a New York Sour.

activities.name: Metro to Batthyány tér, Fő utca lanes
Thermal soak at Rudas or Veli Bej

sections.content: **11:00** Metro to **Batthyány tér**, amble **Fő utca** lanes, climb quiet paths around **Fisherman's Bastion** for arches away from the terraces.
sections.wet: Matthias Church cloisters or shift to House of Music.

sections.heading: Lunch in the Castle District at Pest-Buda Bistro
sections.description: Intimate room, warm service. Goulash then chicken paprikash is the winning move.

sections.title: 15:00-16:30 Golden hour
sections.content: **Buda embankment** for Parliament reflections, then towards **Erzsébet Bridge** lines.
sections.wet: Zwack Unicum Museum and tasting in District IX.

sections.title: 17:30 Thermal soak
sections.content:
**Rudas Baths** - Ottoman dome, rooftop hot pool, central location.

or **Veli Bej (Császár)** - calmer 16th-century hammam feel with timed entries.
sections.note: Choose based on mood and energy.

sections.heading: Dinner at Két Szerecsen
sections.description: Stylish yet relaxed. Duck breast or seafood risotto, both excellent.

sections.heading: Cocktails at Tuk Tuk Bar
sections.description: Tight builds, proper seating. Try Bangkok Nights.

activities.name: Day trip to Szentendre (or Vác if bad weather)
Return to Pest for blue hour
Liberty Bridge and Chain Bridge silhouettes

blocks.title: Getting there
blocks.content: **HÉV H5** from **Batthyány tér** to **Szentendre**, every approximately 20 mins, approximately 39 mins. Use city ticket inside limits plus the **H5 suburban add-on** for Békásmegyer-Szentendre. Buy on BudapestGO or BKK machines.

blocks.title: Loop 11:00-16:00
blocks.content: **Fő tér** cobbles → Serbian Orthodox Church yard textures → meander Ráby and Main Street lanes → **Danube promenade** for long frames → optional **Retro Design Center** for quirky interiors → back via shopfronts.
**Lunch 13:00: Aranysárkány Vendéglő** - venison or duck (warm dining room away from the day-trip clusters).
_Wet-weather tilt: Focus on galleries, Margit Kovács Ceramic Museum, arcades, and a shorter promenade window._

sections.title: Back in Pest by approximately 17:30
sections.content: **Liberty Bridge** silhouettes then **blue hour on the Danube Promenade**.
sections.note: Chain Bridge pedestrian access worth a pass in blue hour.

sections.heading: Dinner 19:30
sections.content:
Return to **Fricska 2.0** or **Rosenstein** as main choices.

Back-ups: Dobrumba, Két Szerecsen.

sections.heading: Cocktail 22:00 at Boutiq'Bar
sections.content: Finish strong with a Smoked Old Fashioned.

blocks.title: Getting there
blocks.content: **MÁV** suburban trains from **Nyugati** about every 20 mins, approximately 35-40 mins.

blocks.title: Compact loop
blocks.content: **Március 15. tér** → Baroque main square and Cathedral facade → **Danube promenade** boardwalk → café stop and backstreets.
Plenty of indoor pauses, fewer crowds, easy return for evening cocktails.

sections.title: For 10:25 flight
sections.content: Walk to **Deák Ferenc tér** and take **100E** to the airport, journey approximately 40 mins. Aim to be at BUD by **08:35** to keep a 90 min buffer. Safe latest leave from Klauzál tér **approximately 07:50**.
**Contingency:** leave approximately 07:30 for extra slack. If the 100E is disrupted, use **200E** via Kőbánya-Kispest, but only if needed.
_Much more civilised departure time. Proper breakfast possible._

sections.title: Important reminders
items: Check out by 10:00 (leave belongings in lockbox if needed)
100E single ticket (2,200 HUF) from Deák Ferenc tér
